segments.text: You have rejected us, God, and burst upon us; you have been angry—now restore us!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You have shaken the land and torn it open; mend its fractures, for it is quaking.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You have shown your people desperate times; you have given us wine that makes us stagger.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But for those who fear you, you have raised a banner to be unfurled against the bow.
Save us and help us with your right hand, that those you love may be delivered.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: God has spoken from his sanctuary:
In triumph I will parcel out Shechem and measure off the Valley of Sukkoth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet, Judah is my scepter.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Moab is my washbasin, on Edom I toss my sandal; over Philistia I shout in triumph.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Is it not you, God, you who have now rejected us and no longer go out with our armies?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Give us aid against the enemy, for human help is worthless.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: With God we will gain the victory, and he will trample down our enemies.
segments.speaker: David
